element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Progressive skill improvement through enemy encounters; two proficiency levels for fighting skills
Hunting wild animals for raw meat (healing resource); teaches resource management
Map shows only current position, no quest markers; forces active exploration and spatial awareness
Choice determines skill trees, magic access levels, and quest progression paths; Old Camp vs New Camp vs Swamp Camp
Mages learn 6 circles; Swamp Camp limited to 4 circles but earlier access; learned from appropriate teachers

element_introduction_order.element: Skill point spending and character progression
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Earned through quests and enemy slaying; spent with specialized teachers for attributes (strength, dexterity, mana) and skills
Single proficiency level; enables alternative approach to combat situations
Two proficiency levels; enables resource acquisition without direct combat
Two proficiency levels; alternative combat approach to melee
Raw meat → grilled meat healing upgrade; weapon forging via NPCs; mimics observed NPC activities
Escalates threat level; requires developed combat skills and possibly magic

typical_relief_triggers: Completing quests and earning skill points
typical_tension_triggers: Exploration of unmapped territory (no quest markers)
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Early game: open-ended exploration with gradual threat escalation; mid game: faction-driven narrative with increasing linearity; combat encounters interspersed with social/quest-based relief periods

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Three distinct faction camps serve as hub structures; exploration paths branch based on camp allegiance and faction control

difficulty_curve.early_game: Low to moderate; untrained protagonist learns basic combat against wild animals; emphasis on exploration and orientation rather than combat challenge
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Moderate to high; faction choice locks certain paths; introduction of magic system and advanced enemies; quest complexity increases
difficulty_curve.late_game: High; quests become linear and mandatory; monster encounters replace exploration freedom; faction differences converge toward singular narrative thread
notable_spikes: Initial encounters with monsters (transition to mid-game)
Faction-locked content becoming unavailable after camp choice

notable_level_patterns.description: Three faction camps (Old Camp, New Camp, Swamp Camp) function as safe hubs with branching exploration zones; players return to hub for training, quests, and resource management
No map available initially; found/purchased maps provide minimal information (position only); forces active environmental learning and spatial memory
Early-game choice between three factions gates access to skills, magic circles, quests, and equipment; creates three substantially different progression paths with convergence in late game
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Camp selection areas (Old Camp, New Camp, Swamp Camp)
